data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-07T01:38:42.111Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating a .NET 10 web API to .NET 11. The app uses EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider.
We have a repository pattern where some methods use synchronous LINQ:

```csharp
public class ProductRepository
{
    private readonly CosmosDbContext _context;

    public List<Product> GetAll()
    {
        return _context.Products.ToList();
    }

    public Product GetById(int id)
    {
        return _context.Products.First(p => p.Id == id);
    }

    public int GetCount()
    {
        return _context.Products.Count();
    }

    public void Save(Product product)
    {
        _context.Products.Add(product);
        _context.SaveChanges();
    }
}
```

In .NET 10 we had the sync-over-async opt-in configured. What breaks in .NET 11?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about what breaks when migrating from .NET 10 to .NET 11 with EF Core using the Azure Cosmos DB provider, specifically around synchronous LINQ operations.

This is about a known breaking change in EF Core. Let me explain what I know:

In EF Core with the Azure Cosmos DB provider, synchronous operations have been problematic because the underlying Azure Cosmos DB SDK is async-only. In earlier versions, EF Core did "sync-over-async" (blocking on async calls) to support sync APIs like `ToList()`, `First()`, `Count()`, `SaveChanges()`, etc.


Starting in EF Core 9, the Cosmos DB provider began throwing exceptions for synchronous operations by default, though you could suppress the warning or configure a context option to allow them. By .NET 11 / EF Core 11, that opt-in mechanism appears to be completely removed, so synchronous operations will throw with no workaround—you'd have to convert everything to async. In .NET 11, that opt-in mechanism gets completely removed, so all synchronous operations on Cosmos DB will throw `InvalidOperationException` with no way around it—the user needs to convert everything to async calls like `ToListAsync()`, `FirstAsync()`, and `SaveChangesAsync()`.
